narration: Start with the widest container so you do not miss a big width.
callout: The width is the distance between the pointers. The height is controlled by the shorter wall.
decision: With heights 3 and 8, the area is 3 x 3 = 9.

narration: The shorter wall is the one limiting the water, so that is the wall that must move.
callout: Moving the taller wall would only make the width smaller while the short wall still blocks the height.
decision: Since 3 is shorter than 8, move the left pointer inward.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Which wall should move after checking heights 3 and 8?

choices.label: Move the left wall
choices.feedback: Correct. The left wall is shorter, so it is the one holding the area back.

choices.label: Move the right wall
choices.feedback: Not this time. The taller wall is not the bottleneck.

narration: After moving the short wall, the width shrinks, but the shorter height may improve enough to help.
callout: Now the walls are 7 and 8, so the new area is 2 x 7 = 14.
decision: Keep saving the best area while the pointers move inward.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is it okay to throw away the shorter wall after you measure one container?
